title: Smoky Arabian Beef Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful stew originating from the Middle East, made with tender beef and aromatic spices.

ingredients.name: Beef (cut into 1-inch cubes)
ingredients.notes: Lean beef or chuck works well

ingredients.name: Smoky Arabian Spice Blend
ingredients.notes: Use a combination of cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and cardamom

ingredients.name: Roasted Garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince the garlic for added depth

ingredients.name: Ginger
ingredients.notes: Grate or slice for added warmth

ingredients.name: Tomato
ingredients.notes: Canned or fresh, diced for added moisture

ingredients.name: Honey
ingredients.notes: A touch of sweetness balances the spice

instructions: Heat oil in the bottom of a large Dutch oven or clay pot over medium-high heat.
Brown the beef cubes in batches until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes. Set aside.
Add more oil if necessary, then sauté the onion, ginger, and garlic until softened and fragrant.
Add the Smoky Arabian Spice Blend and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the tomato, chicken broth, and browned beef. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 1.5 hours or until the beef is tender.
Stir in the honey and cook for an additional 10 minutes.
Serve hot with crusty bread or couscous.
